ref: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.

translated: If you greet someone well, you will get blessings, but if you greet someone badly, you will get curses.

ref: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
text: Ya han bahɛ ya taaba tuure bo' ba, ya Sɔ en boi saazuo ha me wan bahɛ ya tuure bo' ya.
translated: If you do not have your own farm, you can work on someone else's farm so that you can get your own farm.

ref: Your heart will always be where your treasure is. Light (Luke
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, fum lɔgerɔ n boi zi'an la, fum suure boi la bilam. Niho (Luki
translated: If you do good, you will receive good things, if you do bad, you will receive bad things. So (Luke)

ref: You cannot be the slave of two masters! You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Worry (Luke
text: “Nɛra kan ta'am tuna bɔ'ɔra kpɛ'ɛnduma bayi', se'ere en sɔi la, a wan ba'afum yaaɛ ayima gee nɔŋɛ ayima, bii a ba' tuna bɔ'ɔra ayima gee bahera ayima. Ya kan ta'am dɛna yameŋa bo' Yinɛ la lɔgerɔ buyina. Da fabele ya
translated: If you give someone food when they are hungry, and they eat and are satisfied, they will remember you when they are hungry again. But if you give food to someone who is not hungry, they will forget you quickly. So, give help to those who truly need it. That is wisdom.

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Bihɛ ya niihe wa en ɛgeri saazuo wa, ba ka buti bii kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra, bii lageha sũna bã puan, gee ya Sɔ en boi saazuo ha wa en diihe ba. Yamam ka tari tibehum gana bana wa?
translated: If someone gives you something and you don't value it, you may lose it, and when you need it later, you will not find it. Why don't people appreciate what they have?

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
text: Ti yamam en de be'em duma, han mina sɛla en ani suŋa bɔ'ɔra ya kɔma, ŋwani ti ya Sɔ se'em en boi saazuo ha wa, kan bo' sɛba woo en soke en la lɔgesunɔ gana bala. Pa'alesuŋɔ
translated: If you love and respect someone, and you always want good things for them, even when they are not with you, then you have a pure heart and your actions will always bring you blessings. Thank you.
